data_type: # CyberTriage to Timesketch - Windows Data Types Reference

description: computer name stored in the event record.
user security identifier (SID) stored in the event record.

data_type: # Fields added to windows:evtx:record events when --hayabusa is enabled

description: list of tags including hayabusa- sigma- and severity level tags (hayabusa:critical- hayabusa:high- etc.).
rule title(s) that matched (up to 5 rules- with count of additional).
rule ID(s) or file name(s) that matched.
highest severity level of all detections on this event (critical- high- medium- low- informational).
number of Sigma rules that matched this event.
MITRE ATT&CK technique IDs (comma-separated- e.g. attack.t1059.001- attack.execution).
Windows Registry key path for ShellBag entry.
entry last written date and time.
date and time of the last login.
date and time of the last password set.
user ID associated with the startup item.
entry last written date and time.
local user who made the connection.
user ID who accessed the share.
program name as shown in Add/Remove Programs.
True if hidden from Add/Remove Programs.
full package name for Store apps.
entry last written date and time.
date and time the task was last registered.
date and time the task was last launched.
unknown date and time from DynamicInfo.
True if file is a forensic source file.
name of the LNK shortcut file.
target path the shortcut points to.
number of times the program was executed.
number of visits to this URL.
number of visits to this URL.
username associated with the cache entry.
True if command matches suspicious patterns.
job state (Queued- Transferring- Transferred- Error- etc.).
list of WMI consumer types found.
list of suspicious pattern names detected.
sample of surrounding context for analysis.
number of related findings in this event.
size of the deleted file in bytes.
drive number from which file was deleted (C:=3- D:=4- etc.).
full path to $I file in recycle bin.

data_type: # Field names use original Entra camelCase for search compatibility with Azure portal queries.

description: IP address from resource provider if different.
autonomous system number (ASN) for IP address.
client application type (Browser- Mobile Apps and Desktop clients- etc.).
correlation ID for the sign-in event.
conditional access policy result (success- failure- notApplied).
client credential type (none- certificate- secret- etc.).
whether sign-in was flagged for review.
whether sign-in went through Global Secure Access.
original request ID for correlation with related sign-in events.
comma-separated sign-in event types (interactiveUser- nonInteractiveUser- servicePrincipal).

data_type: # Status fields (CRITICAL for blocked login detection)

description: sign-in error code (0=success- 50126=bad password- 53003=CA blocked- 50158=risk blocked).
additional details about the sign-in status.
risk detail from Azure AD Identity Protection.
aggregated risk level (none- low- medium- high- hidden).
risk level at time of sign-in.
risk state (none- confirmedSafe- remediated- dismissed- atRisk- confirmedCompromised).
comma-separated risk event types (unlikelyTravel- unfamiliarFeatures- maliciousIPAddress- etc.).

data_type: # Geolocation fields (CRITICAL for impossible travel analysis)
# Device fields (useful for conditional access analysis)

description: whether device is compliant with policies.
device trust type (Azure AD joined- Azure AD registered- Hybrid Azure AD joined).
JSON array of CA policies with id- displayName- result- enforcedGrantControls- enforcedSessionControls.
JSON array of auth steps with authenticationMethod- succeeded- authenticationStepResultDetail- authenticationStepDateTime.
JSON array with networkType (trustedNamedLocation- etc) and networkNames.
JSON array of key-value pairs with processing context.
JSON array with requirementProvider and detail.

data_type: # Searchable summary fields (extracted from complex arrays)

description: comma-separated names of CA policies that were evaluated (result != notApplied).
comma-separated authentication methods used (Password- Previously satisfied- etc.).

data_type: # ENTRA ID / AZURE AD AUDIT LOGS
# Field names use original Entra camelCase for search compatibility with Azure portal queries.

description: display name of the audit activity (Update user- Add member to group- etc.).
audit log category (UserManagement- GroupManagement- ApplicationManagement- etc.).
service that logged the activity (Core Directory- Self-service Password Management- etc.).
correlation ID for the audit event (can correlate with entra:signin).
target resource type (User- Group- Application- Device- etc.).
JSON array of key-value pairs with additional context (e.g. UserType- Action Client Name).
JSON array of modified properties with displayName- oldValue- newValue (from targetResources[0]).
comma-separated list of property names that were modified.
name of the running process executable.
full path to the process executable.
command line arguments used to launch the process.
True if process has elevated admin privileges.
True if process is running as a Windows service.
number of loaded DLLs/modules in the process.
full path to the loaded DLL/module.
name of the process that loaded this module.
process identifier of the hosting process.
IP address returned by DNS lookup (may be empty if lookup failed).
IP address from ARP cache entry.
MAC/physical address associated with the IP.
next hop gateway address (0.0.0.0 for direct routes).

data_type: #     dhcp_ip_conflict (ID 13) - IP address conflict detected [FORENSIC PRIORITY]
#     dhcp_rogue_unauth (ID 62) - Rogue server not authorized [FORENSIC PRIORITY]
# Vendor Class Reference (common values for device identification):
#   Cisco AP* - Cisco wireless access points

description: client vendor class (e.g. MSFT 5.0- chromeos- android-dhcp-*).
True for security-relevant events (conflicts- denials- rogues).
type of finding (File- process- Web artifact- etc.).
file finding - same fields as ct:analyst:finding.
whether process is a Windows service.

data_type: # MICROSOFT DEFENDER FOR OFFICE 365 (MDO) - URL CLICKS
# Export source: security.microsoft.com > Email & collaboration > Explorer > URL clicks
# CORRELATION: networkMessageId links to mdo:email_event records

description: email address of the user who clicked the URL.
the URL that was clicked (refanged from [%%] format).
extracted domain from the URL for searchability.
GUID linking to source email - CORRELATION KEY with mdo:email_event.
unique identifier for this click event.
IP address of the user when clicking.
JSON array of redirect chain showing full URL path.
client application used (Mail- Microsoft Outlook- OWA- etc.).
user tags (e.g. Priority account- Department Directors).

data_type: # MICROSOFT DEFENDER FOR OFFICE 365 (MDO) - EMAIL EVENTS
# Export source: security.microsoft.com > Email & collaboration > Explorer > All email
# CORRELATION: networkMessageId links to mdo:url_click records
# CORRELATION: senderObjectId/recipientObjectId link to entra:signin userId

description: unique message ID - CORRELATION KEY with mdo:url_click.
envelope sender (MAIL FROM) if different from senderAddress.
sender Entra ID GUID - CORRELATION KEY with entra:signin userId.
recipient Entra ID GUID - CORRELATION KEY with entra:signin userId.
number of URLs in the message.
initial delivery location if different from current.
JSON array of file threats with filename- sha256- threatType- detectionTech.
JSON array of URLs with threatType and refanged url.
final override decision (None- Allowed by policy- etc.).
user-level policy overrides (safe sender lists- etc.).
recipient tags (Priority account- Department Directors- etc.).
Exchange transport rule that was applied.
mail connector used (e.g. Area 1 Inbound Connector).
Data Loss Prevention rule if triggered.
True if this is first contact from sender (spear phishing indicator).
